free_text: Excitation function for the natTi(p,x)48V nuclear
process: Evaluation and new measurements for practical
applications
.Main Reference
Inst. of Nucl. Res., Debrecen,
Hungary, Free University of Brussels, Belgium
Targets were high purity metal foils with natural
isotopic composition made by Goodfellow.
Ti 25.0 and 22.1 microns
Ni 10.809 microns, monitor foils
Cu 10.045 microns, monitor foils
Stacks containing combinations of the
foils were irradiated in Faraday cup for half an hour
with about 200 nA proton beam. The Faraday cup
was equipped with long collimator and electron
suppressor. The primary bombarding energy were 14.7
and 18.0 MeV.
Compiled by Sandor Takacs.
Reactions in subentry 3 corrected. OS
Reaction2 SF8 in subentry 5 corrected. TS
SD: one point in Subent 005 was corrected.
VS decay data corrected in 003.
Uncertainty of the incident deuteron energy
was estimated taking into account the cumulative
cffects of the primary uncertainty sources
incident energy, foil thickness, beam straggling.
The possible energy shift of a representing point in
the excitation function ranges from +-0.3 upto
Total uncertainties of the cross section
values calculated as root-mean-square of all
uncertainties added in quadrature.
The estimated uncertainties on the cross sections
were calculated taking into acount
Beam current                  8%
Nuclear data                  3%
Detector efficiency           5%
Counting statistic less than  1%
Overall uncertainty 10% for data measured in Debrecen
Overall uncertainty 8% for data measured in Brussels
Data were taken from Table 2.
of the original publication
